description: Reciprocating Ipsilateral Bicycle Press This is one we have to be super clear in the demo so members aren’t staring at the screen trying to figure out which leg is bent/extended. It might even be helpful to use the rehearse method: “so is it same said straight or opposite side straight?”  It is also helpful to start off with both the arms and the legs bent, then chowing the same side to straighten up. Last starting the reciprocating process.  If the need we, we can always plant the feet and have them focus on the reciprocating of the chest press solo. #otf #coach #otfcoach #reciprocatingipsilateralbicyclepress #reciprocating

Floor Bridge Hold with Single Arm Close Grip Chest Press The technique may take a little to get comfortable with. There are a few advantages of working single arm at a time: time to focus on symmetrical strength, challenge core balance, use two hands to position and support dumbbell… We are also close to the ground, so a great opportunity for member to try something different/heavier. If members feel unstable we can always do this as a single arm floor press.  #bridgeholdwithsinglearmclosegripchestpress #floorbridgehold #floorbridgeholdwithsinglearmclosegripchestpress #singlearmfloorpress #otf

Low Bench Chest Fly We are looking for functional strength, not bodybuilding. We want wingspan at the bottom of the chest fly. If the weight is causing pressure in the elbow we can use a soft bend. What ever we establish as the angle, should be maintained throughout the movement and not change. We don’t want to change the chest fly for a wide grip chest press which we see often.  #lowbenchchestfly #chestfly #otf #orangetheory #otfcoach
coaching_cues: What ever we establish as the angle, should be maintained throughout the movement and not change

description: Chest Press Set Up for a Drop Set/Reverse Drop Set We want to minimize the transition time when we switch weights with both drop sets and reverse drop sets. Having two sets of dumbbells by your feet can be cumbersome and possibly even dangerous. If space allows, it is helpful to set up on both sides of the bench to maximize space and efficiency in dumbbell weight changes when working heavy.  #dropsets #reversedropsets #reversedropsetup #chestpress #otfcoach

The dumbbell clean (technically a hang power clean). Intention is to express power at the hip on an external load. Use full extention to maximize power output, then retract the hips to sink under the load at a point of it’s apex. Then using the legs to stand the load upright. While there is a shrug and an aggressive extension at the ankles, we generally don’t cue that much. When athletes think about those features it tends to slow the movement and make it choppy.  KISS principle. Stuck to simple cuing; “Jump the weight up and land under,” “jump under the weights,” “tall before you get small”… Be as creative as you want and find something that jives with your speech. #otf #orangetheory #cleans #power #weightlifting

Alternating Single Leg Deadlift We want a soft knee bend and the hips to stay squared forward. One of the faults that we see often is members initiating the hinge, then lifting the leg as almost like an after thought. We want them to work the balance from the beginning, so have them lift the foot up or back before the torso hinges forward.  #alternatingsinglelegdeadlift #singlelegdeadlift #deadlift #balance #posteriorchain

Front squat: The load in the front squat requires a more upright torso positon requiring a bit more quad activation and challenges the core a bit more with the load further from the hips. It stabilizes the load a bit to connect the dumbbells but more challinging core wise to leave them independent. The mini-bands activates glutes a bit more driving the knees out. Bench is just a depth gauge/target. Weight is not entirely in heels but spread evenly in the foot. Most will put too much weight forward in the foot, so we over cue heels.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #frontsquat #squat #fitness

Bent Arm Lateral Raise We see lateral raises often so this bent arm position provides some variance. It also reduces the stress on the wrist and less variability with the position of the elbow.  We want to really watch this position from the front view. To maximize the effectiveness, we want the hands directly in front of the elbows. Often members are going to bring the hands inward. This reduces the overall load brining the weight in from the end of the lever. While we can always use it as an option, to get the most out of this hands would be in directly in front of the elbows.  #bentarmlateralraise #lateralraise #shoulders #delts #deltoids

Bear Plank Hold Same core muscles as the high plank, but a shorter lever. This shorter lever gives us the chance to really focus on the upper body and working protraction. Universally with planks members can choose how much energy they exert. There is low threshold strategies where members are using as little muscle force then they absolute need. Just the opposite would be a high threshold strategy where members feel like they are going to pass out if they hold that force for more than a minute. They can make it as easy or tough as they want.  #bearplank #bearplankhold #otf #coach #orangetheory

High Plank Alternating Hip Tap We are trying to keep the hips steady with shoulder stacked over the hands. If they start with a base too narrow, the hips will sway side to side. Members also often rush the hip tap. Have them move slow, keeping abs tight to control the hips.  While knees is an option, for many elevating the hands to a bend may be a better option.  #highplankalternatinghiptap #plankhiptap #plank #otf #coach
coaching_cues: Have them move slow, keeping abs tight to control the hips

Dumbbell pullover on the bosu. Start off in a great position with the head and shoulders supported. Neck in a relaxed/neutral position. Strong bridge, raising the hips. Bear Plank to Alternating Knee Tap to Push Up Make sure members start off with a wide knee/foot base that allows a more stable position to keep the hold steady when we go through the knee tap.  When it comes to the push up, members often crane their necks, shorting the range of motion. It will help to give them a higher point of focus to keep a neutral neck.  The kneel mat can help to make sure members return to a good bear position. We can also encourage a knee push up if needed for a better range of motion.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #bearplanktoalternatingkneetaptopushup #bearplankkneetappushup

Burpee (No Push Up aka Up Down) Encourage members to try to keep the heels planted when the hands find the ground to relieve pressure in the ankle/knee. Important that we try to maintain a flat back in this explosive movement. Generally can go feet inside hands or feet out but always helpful to land wide on the return.  #burpee #burpeenopushup #updown #otf #coach
coaching_cues: Important that we try to maintain a flat back in this explosive movement

description: Push Up the Alternating Superhero One push up, 5then both sides of the superhero. Slight angles are going to be big changes in this movement. Make sure members start off with a wide base with the feet. Angles are going to be even more particular when it comes to the upperbody. As I was videoing, even the slightest shift forward of my hand made the balance so much more challenging to control slow. Make sure members hands are directly under their shoulder and they move slow and controlled on the superhero balance vs rushing. Even if the push up is done one the knees, they will get a lot out of just lifting hand and opposite leg vs long reaches.  #pushuptoalternatingsuperhero #pushupsuperhero #balance #core #abs

description: Alternating Burpee to Bench Lateral Step Over No push up on the burpee; one burpee one full step over. So if we have the space, burpees are done on both sides of the bench. Side to Side Push Up Very similar to our step out motions we are really working a weight transfer from hand to hand. Normal push up ideal form, neutral spine (probably eye focus 1-2’ forward), chest touching/thighs do not, corkscrew the hands into the ground with a slight tuck of the elbows.  If we use knees as our option, make sure members use the kneel mat to reduce friction/burn on the knees. If members can’t do them from toes but knees are too easy, we can elevate them up to a bench.  #pushups #sidetosidepushups #otf #otfcoach #alternatingaidepushups

Reverse fly (w/mid band): Important that we keep this tempo smooth and under control, especially the eccentric phase or lowering movement. Too upright of a torso engages traps. No bodybuilder bounces with too heavy of weight which overload stabilizers control of the weights. Lateral Bound to Reverse Lunge Plyometrics are generally categorized into either short response or long response. Lateral bounds are a long response where we take time to load, explode, and then absorb force.  To keep members successful start off with a small distance to get used of the balance it requires in the lateral movement. As they move through the timed section, ideally they would open up more range still controlling the balance. Alternating Neutral Grip Push Press The whole intention of the dip is to gain a little power but the shoulder is still working a good amount. The depth of the dip is dependent on how much help the shoulder needs. If the weight is easy to moderate, the dip is shallow. The more fatigue that sets into the shoulder, the deeper the dip should get. Members should choose a weight too heavy to do multiple reps of a shoulder press. A good option would just to do an alternating explosive shoulder press.  #alternatingneutralgrippushpress #dumbbellalternatingpushpress #pushpress #shoulders #otf

description: Biceps Curl to Shoulder Press Solely in the curl we really focus on the importance of elbow position as it changes the leverage/momentum arm. Since this is a combination movement, it’s ok if the elbow moves forward of the torso since we are transitioning into a shoulder press. I follow the VVA but it is helpful to do both palms facing forward and palms rotating inwards when it comes to curls. For the shoulder press, idea palms face forward with elbow to the side. For some elbow in front of torso will help, for other palms fully facing in on a neutral grip. Last, if member are tight through the shoulders or are having a hard time bracing the core, we can always offer a staggered stance to help. #bicepscurltoshoulderpress #curltopress #combinationmovement #otf #coach

Single Leg Shoulder Press This movement is going to be a ton more challenging for members than it seems, especially with cushioned running shoes with a high stack height. Might have to remind members: balance only gets better if we work on it. Focal points matter! If they use the toe down let’s be strategic on placement and remind them to only use it as much as they need. If we use lateral raises or scaption, just keep in mind center of mass is changing and balance may be more difficult.  #singlelegshoulderpress #balanceshoulderpress #balance #otf #otfcoach

Seated Reciprocating Shoulder Press Reciprocating is going to require coordination and stabilization. The seated posting is going to restrict any contribution from the legs as we fatigue. As it reciprocates, it may create some space in the AC joint for some, if there is still pinching we can try neutral grip or his go to a lateral raise. It’s important for us as coaches to demo heavy so they know it should be a struggle. It doesn’t have to be your true weight but it should be heavy. It is also important that we know how to explain how to get setup for heavy safely and teach them even if they don’t need it yet. #seatedreciprocatingshoulderpress #shoulderpress #seatedshoulderpress #orangetheory #otfcoach

Front Loaded Contralateral Single Arm Split Squat (Slow) In all split squats we want the feet to be about shoulder width apart. We want majority of weight in the front leg, maybe 70-80% of weight. Especially as we work a slow descent we might need to slightly tip the torso forward for better weight distribution. When it comes to that deliberate slow count, often members push too much weight in the back leg. We need constant reminders to be heavy in the front leg.  It is much more challenging at tempo with up high and away from the hips, if members are having a hard time shifting weight forward, we can always have them use the option of weight down by their side. If they have balance issues, we can always move to ipsilateral loading as well.  #otf #coach #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquatslow #frontloadedcontralateralsinglearmsplitsquat #balance

Squat Jump with Elbows Wide It’s always important that we have a “why”? The elbows wide, aka prisoner squat position, lifts the chest and keeps the back upright and slightly extended. We want members to shoot for height in their jumps and be as explosive as possible. Important that we coach a quiet landing as well, teaching them how to muscularly absorb force. If they are not jumpers, we still want them as explosive as possible but keeping their toes on the ground.  #otf #coach #otfcoach #squatjumpwithelbowswide #prisonersquatjump

Single Arm Clean Technically should be titled a hang power clean. (Hang: starting position, power: noting it is received in a high squat vs a full squat) this movement is all about a big violent hip extension followed by a rapid small retract, so an opening and closing of the hip.  Best thing we can do is to simplifying for the members; jump the weight up to shoulder height, and land in a baby squat. Just jump and land. This has nothing to do with a curl and I dislike it as an option personally. If member need an option find something to mirror or mimic and aggressive hip opening (ideally followed by a quick close, but at least the hip extension) #hangpowerclean #singlearmhangpowerclean #singlearmclean #dumbbellclean #power

Refining the transverse squat! We do a lot of saggital plane movements and even some frontal plane but not too much in the transverse plane.  1. Don’t plant the foot then squat. If you plant the foot then squat. The tranverse plane only worked to get the foot into place, then you are doing a regular squat. Think rotate and squat simultaneously.  2. If the knee collapses inside of the big toe, we get excessive stress on the lateral stabilizers of the base leg knee. Make sure knee tracks 2nd/3rd toe. You can always step around a less agressive angle to control knee tracking.  3. If the base foot moves, we are taking away from the tranverse plane and getting closer to a transverse lunge vs a squat. Glue that foot in place.  4. Stepping back in-line with the base leg puts excessive rotation at either the knee or hip. Make sure to step back and out. You can always use a bench as a marker to ensure you are stepping out and around.  Enjoy and keep those knees safe!  #otf #orangetheory #coach #tranversesquat #transverseplane #squat

Make sure to step back and out

BOSU Bridge Hold with Cross Body Triceps Extension (Feet On) Great combination movement. The bridge hold engages core and posterior chain, while upper body isolates a triceps extension. Members are most likely going to have a hard time only hinging from the elbow into the cross body position and not moving the upper arm. It will help to cue to keep the elbow over the shoulder as you bring your thumb to the opposite armpit.  #bosubridgeholdwithcrossbodytricepsextension #tricepsextensions #triceps #bosu #otfcoach

Y-Raise: Most OTF exercises ask for shoulder blades to be back and down (retracted and depressed). This is one of the few movements that actually call for scapular elevation and upward rotation. If we get members into a great starting position, it doesn’t take an aggressive angle to challenge the movement. Let’s have them actively drive the hands away from the shoulders. Always slight tension on the straps, no saggy straps. Core and bodyline rigid, no bends at the elbows and control the eccentric/lowering phase.  #otf #orangetheory #coach #yraises #trx #fitness
